titleStmt.title: Letter from Edward O'Farrell to William F. Hearn, 27 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Edward O'Farrell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Sir Edward O'Farrell informing Hearn that the return of his client's Ford motor car has been authorised by the General Officer Commanding-in-Chief the Forces of Ireland. Sir Edward O'Farrell (c.1857-1926) served as the Assistant Under Secretary to the Lord Lieutenant of Ireland, Sir Ivor Guest Churchill, Lord Wimborne.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, NAI-CSO-RP-1916-10506-2

correspAction.placeName: Charles Street, New Ross, Wexford, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
27th June 1916.
10506. Sir, With reference to your letter of the 21st
instant, respecting a Ford motor car the property
of Rev. Michael Murphy, C.C., of Taghmon, County
Wexford, I am directed by the Lords of Justices to
inform you that the General Officer Commanding-in-
Chief the Forces in Ireland has authorised the
return of this motor car to the owner. I am
Sir,
Your obedient Servant, E O Farrell William F. Hearn, Esq., L L.D. Solicitor, Charles Street, New Ross.
